_meta.title: Chart/data-visualization family — design INTAKE (scope ruling + first records; no build)
_meta.author: system-planner
_meta.grounding: Kim's artifact-feed ask ('a report as a chart or graph') · B6 bridge LLD §10 fork 2 (deferred as its own PRD-level intake) · CLAUDE.md zero-dep pillar · references/geometry.md five size-classes · ADR-0087 whole-fleet catalog gate (SPEC-N2) · ADR-0097 ask-feed partition · ADR-0065 pure-core+subpath precedent
_meta.note: Both planes derived independently: OUTSIDE-IN structure from the intake record family (scope ruling -> contract directions -> ecosystem dispositions -> records -> gates/handback); INSIDE-OUT actions from the dispatch's scope questions (types, data shape, rendering, a11y, sizing, catalog/teaching) + the standing doctrine set (zero-dep, geometry law, SPEC-N2 gate, ADR-0097 boundary, handoff contract).

nodes.label: v1 scope ruling (types in + types ruled out, reasons from mechanics)
nodes.accept: the PRD scope section names the v1-IN types AND the ruled-out set with a per-item reason; harness_checks.py prd P3 (out-of-scope stated) passes as part of exit 0

nodes.label: contract directions (the what-direction the ADR records; SPEC/LLD own the mechanism later)
data-contract direction (the prop shape a model can emit as JSON)
nodes.accept: ADR-0107 Decision states each v1 type's data prop name + shape + one verbatim JSON example an agent could emit

nodes.label: rendering direction (zero-dep mechanism per mark type)
nodes.accept: ADR-0107 Decision names the render mechanism per v1 type, cites the zero-dep pillar, and no third-party charting library appears anywhere except as a rejected Alternative

nodes.label: a11y contract direction (what a chart announces)
nodes.accept: ADR-0107 Decision states default role + accessible-name behavior per v1 type via ElementInternals (never host attributes), naming the ui-icon decorative-default prior art it deliberately inverts

nodes.label: size-class placement under the geometry law
nodes.accept: ADR-0107 Decision names the geometry.md size class each v1 type joins (or justifies a new class) and the --ui-{name}-* token family for mark geometry

nodes.label: catalog + Gen-UI teaching disposition (SPEC-N2 / ADR-0087 compliance path + exemplar seed)
nodes.accept: ADR-0107 Decision states the same-wave catalog-row-or-allowlist path (end-of-wave allowlist residue: none) and names the report-card exemplar seed that upgrades the stats-grid idiom

nodes.label: packaging ruling (pure-core+subpath applicability)
nodes.accept: ADR-0107 records the package ruling in the Decision and carries the @agent-ui/charts leaf-package option in Alternatives with the reason it loses

nodes.label: feed-policy disposition (ADR-0097 ask partition boundary)
nodes.accept: ADR-0107 records whether FEED_SURFACE_TYPES gains chart types, with a reason grounded in the ask-vs-report surface split

nodes.label: PRD-sibling-vs-extension ruling + the compact PRD (.claude/docs/prd/chart-family.prd.md)
nodes.accept: the PRD file exists under .claude/docs/prd/ with stable PRD-G IDs, metric+baseline+target+timeframe per goal, both-sided scope, priority tiers, and the sibling-vs-extension ruling stated in its header; python /Users/kimba/.claude/skills/prd-author/scripts/harness_checks.py prd <path> exits 0

nodes.label: scoping ADR-0107 (proposed; Kim forks with firm recommendations)
nodes.accept: 0107-*.md exists under .claude/docs/adr/ with Status: proposed, non-empty Repairs, all four required sections + Acceptance, and >=2 named Kim forks each carrying a firm recommendation; python /Users/kimba/.claude/skills/adr-author/scripts/adr_check.py <path> exits 0

nodes.label: index rows (adr/README.md)
nodes.accept: adr/README.md index table carries a 0107 row (title + status + repairs); adr_check C6 (indexed) passes

nodes.label: mechanical harness gates run to green
nodes.accept: harness_checks.py prd exits 0 AND adr_check.py exits 0, both actually run this session with output read (never eyeballed)

nodes.label: design-status handback (handoff-compose contract)
nodes.accept: the final handback carries the eight handoff-compose fields in order, Status on line 1, gate verdicts by command, and names doc-reviewer as the ratifying critic

actions.label: decide which chart types earn v1 and which are ruled out, argued from mechanics not fashion
decide the data shape a model can emit (number[] vs {label,value}[] weighed per type)
decide the rendering mechanism per mark under the zero-dep pillar (SVG vs CSS)
decide what each chart announces to assistive tech + the non-color-signifier posture
place the chart types in the five-size-class geometry taxonomy
disposition the catalog rows + the Gen-UI exemplar under the whole-fleet gate
rule whether chart primitives warrant the pure-core+subpath package split
disposition the ADR-0097 ask-feed policy against chart types
rule PRD-extension-vs-sibling and author the compact PRD with gated goals
record the scope decision as a proposed ADR naming the genuine Kim forks
index the new records so every citation resolves
run the mechanical doc gates and fix to green
compose the handback naming the reviewer seat

edges.why: the PRD's scope section pins the v1-in/out ruling — it cannot be authored before the ruling exists
ADR-0107's Decision clause 1 records the type-set ruling
the ADR records the data-contract direction; it must be decided before the record is written
the ADR records the rendering direction; deciding it is prerequisite content
the ADR records the a11y contract direction; deciding it is prerequisite content
the ADR records the size-class placement; deciding it is prerequisite content
the catalog/exemplar disposition is ADR Decision content
the packaging ruling is ADR Decision/Alternatives content
the feed-policy disposition is ADR content
the ADR's Repairs field pins the PRD and its clauses cite PRD-G IDs — the PRD must exist first
the index row cites the ADR's number + title
the PRD gate runs over the authored PRD
the ADR gate runs over the authored ADR
adr_check C6 asserts the README index row exists — the gate can only pass after indexing
the handback reports gate verdicts by command — the gates must have run first
